channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Manned Fighter Jets to Dominate Air Combat for Next Two Decades: DRDO Chief Samir V. Kamat Outlines Transition to Drone-Centric Warfare
item.description: At the core of this outlook is the continued necessity of a “human-in-the-loop” in complex combat environments. Despite rapid advances in artificial intelligence, modern air warfare involves split-second decision-making under uncertain and highly dynamic conditions, particularly in contested environments with electronic warfare, degraded communications, and ambiguous target identification. Critical decisions—such as engaging high-value or civilian-adjacent targets—still require human judgment, accountability, and adaptability, something current autonomous systems cannot fully replicate.
item.dc:creator: IDRW.ORG

item.title: S-400 Operator Explained how PAF AWACS was taken down inside Pakistan airspace
item.description: In a candid interview with Face to Face, an initiative led by Dr. Syed Rizwan Ahmed, Singh recounted his experience operating the S-400 system during Operation Sindoor. He revealed that during the engagement phase, a hostile aircraft blip was detected nearly 300 km inside Pakistani airspace.
item.dc:creator: IDRW.ORG

item.title: AMCA Programme Gains Momentum as MoD Prepares RFP for Private Sector Participation
item.description: India’s flagship fifth-generation fighter programme, the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA), is moving into a critical procurement phase, with Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh confirming that a Request for Proposal (RFP) will soon be issued to shortlisted private sector players. The announcement signals a major structural shift in India’s aerospace manufacturing strategy, placing the private industry at the center of next-generation fighter production.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Seeks 360° Situational Awareness System with AI Driver Assistance for Protected Mobility Vehicles
item.description: The Indian Army has identified a critical operational gap in battlefield mobility and is seeking the development of an advanced 360° Situational Awareness Kit with integrated driver assistance for Protected Mobility Vehicles (PMVs) under the DISC 14 innovation challenge. The requirement reflects the growing need to enhance visibility, safety, and decision-making for vehicle crews operating in complex and high-risk environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Saab’s Composite Superstructure Technology: How Carbon Fibre is Redefining Naval Stealth and Ship Design for India
item.description: Saab’s carbon fibre composite superstructure technology represents a quiet but significant shift in modern naval architecture, moving away from conventional steel and aluminium toward lighter, more survivable, and inherently stealthier designs. Originally proven on Sweden’s Visby-class corvettes, this approach has already found a foothold in the Indian Navy through platforms like INS Kavaratti and INS Kiltan, underscoring its operational relevance beyond niche experimentation.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: PD-8 Engine Crosses 6,100 Test Hours as Russia Pushes Toward 2026 Certification Milestones for India sale
item.description: Russia’s PD-8 turbofan engine program has entered an advanced stage of development, with cumulative operating time surpassing 6,100 hours across ground bench testing and flight trials. The milestone, disclosed by United Engine Corporation (UEC), reflects an intensive certification campaign that now spans both laboratory validation and real-world flight environments, including operations on prototype Superjet aircraft and the Il-76LL flying testbed.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Not Just Kurt Tank: India Also Invited Legendary Willy Messerschmitt to Shape Its Post-Independence Aviation Future
item.description: In the early years after Independence, India actively sought expertise from some of the world’s foremost aircraft designers to build a self-reliant aviation industry. While the story of German designer Kurt Tank’s contributions to the HF-24 Marut is well known, newly highlighted historical records reveal that the legendary Professor Willy Messerschmitt — the man behind the iconic Me-109 and Me-262 — was also formally approached by the Indian government.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Ravi K Assumes Charge As HAL’s CMD
item.description: He brings over 30 years of experience across various sectors such as A&D, manufacturing and electronics. Prior to this, he was the Director (Operations) at HAL, where he led strategic planning and played a key role in securing HAL’s Maharatna status.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: India’s New Strategic Maritime Hub Takes Shape At Great Nicobar
item.description: The project, which will strengthen India’s strategic presence in the Andaman Sea and Southeast Asia, seeks to balance port-led economic growth with calibrated environmental safeguards and protection of indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: India, China Offer Contrasting Styles In Dealing With Neighbours Amid Oil Crisis
item.description: When China offers energy, as it did with Taiwan, the offer arrives bundled with a political price tag attached to it. When India supplies fuel to Nepal or Sri Lanka, it does so through existing intergovernmental frameworks, with no conditions on sovereignty or political alignment, according to an article in Daily Mirror Online.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Prez Murmu Visits Army Training Command In Shimla, Appreciates Role In Fostering Innovation
item.description: She was received by Himachal Pradesh Governor Kavinder Gupta and Lieutenant General Devendra Sharma, GOC-in-C, ARTRAC, on her arrival at the training command headquarters. The President was briefed by GOC-in-C on the evolution of ARTRAC, its role in providing quality training to Indian Army personnel to enhance operational efficiency, as well as on salient initiatives of ARTRAC, said a statement.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: J&K Police lodge FIR as Syed Ali Shah Geelani’s video surfaces online
item.description: The move comes a day after Peoples Democratic Party leader Iltija Mufti posted a video of the late hardline separatist leader on X.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Silent on Ladakhi people’s demands of statehood, Sixth Schedule status: Congress slams Shah
item.description: Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh recalled India’s first Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru’s July 1949 visit to Ladakh.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Who is Lt Gen Balbir Singh, the new commander of Srinagar-based XV Corps
item.description: Lt Gen Singh had earlier commanded the Victor Force, which looks after counter-insurgency operations in south and central Kashmir. A statement from the XV Corps, often referred to as Chinar Corps, said Lt Gen Singh is a highly decorated and battle-hardened officer with over 34 years of service.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Two men get jail term for hoisting Khalistani flag on government building in Punjab: NIA
item.description: The accused, identified as Inderjeet Singh and Jaspal Singh, residents of Moga, have been sentenced to five years and six months in jail, along with a fine of Rs 16,000 each, by the court at SAS Nagar in Mohali, a statement issued by the NIA said.
item.dc:creator: PTI
